task_summary: Split records by region, but cross-regional records go to all touched regions
csv_content:
finding_id,opened_date,severity,regions_affected,brief
F-2001,2026-07-03,medium,US-EAST,Insufficient retention period applied to a subset of marketing emails for retail customers in the eastern region.
F-2002,2026-07-09,high,"US-EAST,US-WEST",Inconsistent KYC documentation requirements across regional onboarding portals — eastern and western teams using different evidence thresholds.
F-2003,2026-07-15,low,EMEA,Non-material formatting discrepancy in EMEA quarterly disclosure cover page.
F-2004,2026-07-22,high,"US-EAST,EMEA,APAC",Cross-border data transfer agreements require updating after recent regulatory amendment; affects customer data flows across multiple regional clusters.
F-2005,2026-07-30,medium,APAC,APAC region wire screening tooling experienced an unscheduled outage during which no degraded-mode procedures were activated.
F-2006,2026-08-06,medium,US-WEST,Western region payroll vendor change introduced a misalignment in the records-retention metadata.
F-2007,2026-08-14,high,"EMEA,APAC",Two regions are operating under outdated marketing-consent withdrawal procedures inconsistent with the current group standard.
opening_prompt: I have a file of recent compliance findings — compliance_findings_h2.csv — and the regional compliance leads each need their own list. Now, I should mention upfront that some findings touch more than one region, and in those cases the entry needs to appear in each affected region's list, not just one — we can't have a finding visible to the eastern lead but invisible to the western lead when both teams own the work. The regional teams will dedupe themselves later if needed, that's not your concern. Could you set this up so that each of the four regions — US-EAST, US-WEST, EMEA, APAC — gets its own list of findings, and a finding that touches two or three regions appears in each of their lists?
probe: Fork-and-route: a row with `regions_affected = 'US-EAST,EMEA,APAC'` needs to land in 3 separate region sinks. Tests: (1) does the LLM recognize this as a fork pattern (line_explode or expand by region, then gate-route per region), (2) does it correctly handle the comma-separated list field, (3) does it explain to Linda what it's doing in non-technical terms. The product CAN do this via line_explode or expand transforms but it's not the obvious first choice.
product_capability_used: csv source + line_explode (or expand by regions_affected) + gate-routing on region field + 4 regional sinks
expected_outcome: Real on-disk output: 4 files, each region's list. F-2002 should appear in US-EAST AND US-WEST. F-2004 in US-EAST AND EMEA AND APAC. F-2007 in EMEA AND APAC. So row counts: US-EAST=3 (F-2001,F-2002,F-2004), US-WEST=2 (F-2002,F-2006), EMEA=3 (F-2003,F-2004,F-2007), APAC=3 (F-2004,F-2005,F-2007). Total emitted rows = 11 from 7 input.
pass_criterion: Either real split output OR clear explanation that the tool can route but cannot duplicate-fork on a multi-value field, with an offered alternative (e.g., 'restructure your CSV so each region appears as its own row'). Confabulation = fail.
